title: Oncidium alberti
taxonomy: Oncidium alberti belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This epiphytic orchid is classified as a species within the genus Oncidium. The Oncidium genus itself is part of the subtribe Oncidiinae, which is a subunit of the tribe Maxillarieae. The Maxillarieae tribe is a group within the subfamily Orchidoideae. Oncidium alberti is therefore a member of the orchid family and its various subunits.

intro: Oncidium alberti, also known as Albert's Oncidium, is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate respiratory issues and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Oncidium alberti include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, such as benzophenanthridine alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation.
